pages.raw_text:
No palmer citation as to the fact
of Sheriff Doak's trucking to the
Washington Water Power company
can be pointed out than the remark
he made to the delegation of line
men who appealed to Doak to call
off his temporary deputies chosen
from the ranks of the company's
employees.


The linemen pointed out to Doak
the injustice of his investing en-
ployes of the company with county
police authority at the time when
other employees were striking. The
delegation asked the sheriff to re-
call the stars he had give out. The
reply made was, in effect:


II can't do it. because those men
have been appointed for two years"


That reply indicates clearly that
for two years, which have not yet
expired, Sheriff Dcak has tied him.
self to this corporation.


Why did he do it?


1t also indicates that the cOrpOr-
alton has insinuated itself into the
good graces of a county official.


How has 1t done it?


Why should Sheriff Doak, or any
other sheriff. invest employees of the
Washington Water Power company,
or any other company, with powers
of the county which he cannot re-
call under two years?


The treasurer might as we'll in
vest that company, or any other,
with some of the authority of the
treasury; and from talk in certain
quarters, he has allowed public
funds to be used for the benefit of
certain ones.


The prosecuting attorney might
loan his legal lorse to others, and
it is said that here has come in his
work as protective advisor" let
alone selling his talented services
to School District. 1875 for los.


Officials cannot serve the public
faithfully and hand any favors out
to corporations.
